seoTitle: Add Custom Fonts to Your HubSpot Site (Brand Kit)
metaDescription: Upload proprietary web fonts to HubSpot's Brand Kit, map font weights, and apply your custom typography across pages, blogs, and landing pages.
intro: Instead of editing individual page templates, HubSpot's Brand Kit lets you upload custom fonts once and apply them consistently across your whole site. This guide covers uploading .woff2 font files, defining weights and styles, and swapping in your typography through the theme editor. It ends with setting up fallback fonts so your layout stays stable if something fails to load.

steps.heading: Open Branding settings
steps.body: Go to Account Defaults in HubSpot settings and open the Branding tab.

steps.heading: Access the Brand Kit fonts section
steps.body: Click My Brand Kit and expand the Core Fonts section.

steps.heading: Upload your font files
steps.body: Select Add Font, then Upload Custom Font, and drag your .woff2 files into the drop zone.

steps.heading: Set the font display name
steps.body: Input an official display name for the font so it's identifiable when applying it later.

steps.heading: Map font weights
steps.body: Assign weight values, such as 400 for regular and 700 for bold, to save accurate style profiles.

steps.heading: Apply and publish
steps.body: Open your theme's Global Styles panel, select the new brand font, review the preview, and publish.

faq.question: What font format does HubSpot's Brand Kit accept?
faq.answer: It's built around .woff2 files, which offer faster load times and broad browser support.

faq.question: Will custom fonts apply site-wide automatically?
faq.answer: Once mapped through the theme's Global Styles panel and published, the font applies across the site.

faq.question: Why should I set a fallback font?
faq.answer: A fallback family like Arial or sans-serif keeps your layout structurally stable if the custom font fails to load.

faq.question: Can I set different weights for the same font?
faq.answer: Yes, you can map specific weight values, like 400 for regular and 700 for bold, when uploading.
